steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The task is to make AND support a claim that kale is a better source of vitamin C than blueberries. A correct answer must both assert kale's superiority and provide quantitative evidence from the notes to back it up.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Kale contains ascorbic acid (which humans cannot make in their bodies), and blueberries do too.": Mentions both foods but makes no comparative claim and provides no quantitative support. ✗
  • "Blueberries contain 10 mg of vitamin C per 100 g, but kale is a better source of vitamin C; in fact, kale contains 120 mg per 100 g.": Explicitly claims kale is better and supports it with the 120 mg vs. 10 mg comparison from the notes. ✓
  • "Kale is a better source of vitamin C, also known as ascorbic acid, than blueberries are.": Makes the claim but offers no supporting evidence — the notes' numbers are absent. ✗
  • "Humans must get vitamin C from foods like kale and blueberries because they cannot make it in their bodies.": Focuses on why vitamin C is essential, not on comparing the two sources. Makes no comparative claim at all. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
